--- title: "PK Analysis" subtitle: "Introduction to `{ospsuite}`" module: "Introduction to `{ospsuite}`" ID: A09 date: last-modified institute: | ![](../resources/esqlabs.svg){height=100} format: esqlabs-revealjs --- ::: {.r-fit-text} ## **ūüéĮ Goal: Learn to compute pharmacokinetic parameters of a simulation** ::: ::: {.cell} ::: ## PK-Analysis View in PK-Sim :::center-x ![](https://881660647-files.gitbook.io/~/files/v0/b/gitbook-legacy-files/o/assets%2F-LNl6UIiFP7k0sNQthlR%2F-LQJPSoNjgtDlxBl9HHq%2F-LOWQ5AI-r7-sKUIoPqE%2Fpk-analysis-20-17.jpg?generation=1541162017853367&alt=media) Also available using `{ospsuite}` ! ::: ## Available PK parameters depend on the simulation type | Simulation Type | PK Parameters | |-----------------|--------------------------------------------| | All | AUC, Cmax, Cend, tmax, Half-Life, MRT, ... | | IV | Vss, Vd, CL, Body clearance, ... | | Oral | Vss/F, Vd/F, CL/F, Fraction absorbed, ... | | Multiple adm. | AUC, Cthrough_dDi, ... | :::center-x Full list: [docs.open-systems-pharmacology.org](https://docs.open-systems-pharmacology.org/working-with-pk-sim/pk-sim-documentation/pk-sim-simulations#pk-parameters) ::: :::notes - MRT: Mean residence time - Vss: Volume of distribution at steady state - Vd: Volume of distribution - CL: Clearance - F: Bioavailability - Cthrough: Trough concentration just before the i-th dose is administered ::: ## Overview 1. Compute PK parameters 2. Access PK parameters 3. Import and export 4. Customize PK parameters # Compute PK Parameters ## Calculated from Simulation Results - PK parameters can be calculated for all outputs of a simulation. - Pass a `SimulationResults` object to `calculatePKAnalyses()`. ```r pkAnalysis <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simulationResults) ``` ## Get simulation results ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} # Load the package library(ospsuite) # Load the simulation sim <- loadSimulation(system.file( "extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite" )) # Run the simulation simulationResults <- runSimulations(sim)[[1]] ``` ::: ## Calculate PK Parameters ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} pkAnalysis <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simulationResults) pkAnalysis ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` For outputs:: ‚ÄĘ Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Organism|VenousBlood|Plasma|Aciclovir|Plasma Unbound For pK-Parameters:: ‚ÄĘ C_max ‚ÄĘ C_max_norm ‚ÄĘ t_max ‚ÄĘ C_tEnd ‚ÄĘ AUC_tEnd ‚ÄĘ AUC_tEnd_norm ‚ÄĘ AUC_inf ‚ÄĘ AUC_inf_norm ‚ÄĘ MRT ‚ÄĘ Thalf ‚ÄĘ FractionAucLastToInf ‚ÄĘ CL ‚ÄĘ Vss ‚ÄĘ Vd ``` ::: ::: ::: {.callout-note} `calculatePKAnalyses()` returns a `SimulationPKAnalyses` R6 object. ::: # Explore PK Parameters ## Available Output Paths & Parameters ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} pkAnalysis$allQuantityPaths ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)" [2] "Organism|VenousBlood|Plasma|Aciclovir|Plasma Unbound" ``` ::: ::: . . . ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} pkAnalysis$allPKParameterNames ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "C_max" "C_max_norm" "t_max" [4] "C_tEnd" "AUC_tEnd" "AUC_tEnd_norm" [7] "AUC_inf" "AUC_inf_norm" "MRT" [10] "Thalf" "FractionAucLastToInf" "CL" [13] "Vss" "Vd" ``` ::: ::: ## All Parameters for One Output Path Use `allPKParametersFor()` to retrieve all PK parameters for a given output path. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} pkAnalysis$allPKParametersFor( quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [[1]] ‚ÄĘ Name: C_max ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: Concentration (molar) ‚ÄĘ Unit: ¬Ķmol/l [[2]] ‚ÄĘ Name: C_max_norm ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: Concentration (mass) ‚ÄĘ Unit: kg/l [[3]] ‚ÄĘ Name: t_max ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: Time ‚ÄĘ Unit: min [[4]] ‚ÄĘ Name: C_tEnd ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: Concentration (molar) ‚ÄĘ Unit: ¬Ķmol/l [[5]] ‚ÄĘ Name: AUC_tEnd ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: AUC (molar) ‚ÄĘ Unit: ¬Ķmol*min/l [[6]] ‚ÄĘ Name: AUC_tEnd_norm ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: AUC (mass) ‚ÄĘ Unit: kg*min/l [[7]] ‚ÄĘ Name: AUC_inf ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: AUC (molar) ‚ÄĘ Unit: ¬Ķmol*min/l [[8]] ‚ÄĘ Name: AUC_inf_norm ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: AUC (mass) ‚ÄĘ Unit: kg*min/l [[9]] ‚ÄĘ Name: MRT ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: Time ‚ÄĘ Unit: min [[10]] ‚ÄĘ Name: Thalf ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: Time ‚ÄĘ Unit: min [[11]] ‚ÄĘ Name: FractionAucLastToInf ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: Fraction [[12]] ‚ÄĘ Name: CL ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: Flow per weight ‚ÄĘ Unit: l/min/kg [[13]] ‚ÄĘ Name: Vss ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: Volume per body weight ‚ÄĘ Unit: l/kg [[14]] ‚ÄĘ Name: Vd ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: Volume per body weight ‚ÄĘ Unit: l/kg ``` ::: ::: ## One Parameter Use `pKParameterFor()` to retrieve a single PK parameter for a given output path. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} oneParam <- pkAnalysis$pKParameterFor( quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)", pkParameter = "C_max" ) oneParam ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ‚ÄĘ Name: C_max ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: Concentration (molar) ‚ÄĘ Unit: ¬Ķmol/l ``` ::: ::: . . . Each returned object is a `QuantityPKParameter` with fields `$name`, `$quantityPath`, `$dimension`, `$unit`, and `$values`. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} oneParam$values ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 50.25272 ``` ::: ::: ## Get a Data Frame `pkAnalysesToDataFrame()` returns all PK parameters for all output paths as a data frame. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} pkAnalysisDF <- pkAnalysesToDataFrame(pkAnalysis) pkAnalysisDF[, c("QuantityPath", "Parameter", "Value", "Unit")] ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: ::: {.callout-note} Columns: `IndividualId`, `QuantityPath`, `Parameter`, `Value`, `Unit`. ::: # Import and Export ## CSV Interoperability - PK parameters can be exported and imported through `.csv` files. - These files are interoperable between PK-Sim and `{ospsuite}`. ## Import ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} # example file PKAnalysisFilePath <- system.file( "extdata", "PKAnalyses.csv", package = "ospsuite" ) # import to ospsuite framework pkAnalysis <- importPKAnalysesFromCSV( filePath = PKAnalysisFilePath, simulation = sim ) ``` ::: ::: {.callout-warning} The related simulation must be loaded to import the PK parameters. ::: ## Export ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} exportPKAnalysesToCSV(pkAnalyses = pkAnalysis, filePath = "PKAnalysis.csv") ``` ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: # User-Defined PK Parameters ## Custom PK parameters - Useful when working on different time intervals or dimensions other than concentrations - Based on existing PK parameters (listed in `ospsuite::StandardPKParameter`) - Custom formulas are not supported ```r addUserDefinedPKParameter(name, standardPKParameter) ``` ## Sample of `StandardPKParameter` `ospsuite::StandardPKParameter` exposes the available base parameters: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} names(ospsuite::StandardPKParameter) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] "Unknown" "C_max" "C_max_norm" [4] "C_min" "C_min_norm" "t_max" [7] "t_min" "C_trough" "C_trough_norm" [10] "AUC_tEnd" "AUC_tEnd_norm" "AUCM_tEnd" [13] "AUC_inf" "AUC_inf_norm" "AUC_tEnd_inf" [16] "AUC_tEnd_inf_norm" "CL" "MRT" [19] "FractionAucEndToInf" "Thalf" "Vss" [22] "Vd" "Tthreshold" ``` ::: ::: ## Create a New Parameter ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} myAUC <- addUserDefinedPKParameter( name = "MyAUC", standardPKParameter = StandardPKParameter$AUC_tEnd ) myAUC ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ‚ÄĘ Name: MyAUC ‚ÄĘ DisplayName: MyAUC ‚ÄĘ Dimension: AUC (molar) ‚ÄĘ DisplayUnit: ¬Ķmol*min/l ‚ÄĘ StandardPKParameter: AUC_tEnd ``` ::: ::: ## Time Interval ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} # Specifies start time and end time in minutes myAUC$startTime <- 50 myAUC$endTime <- 80 myAUC ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ‚ÄĘ Name: MyAUC ‚ÄĘ DisplayName: MyAUC ‚ÄĘ Dimension: AUC (molar) ‚ÄĘ DisplayUnit: ¬Ķmol*min/l ‚ÄĘ StandardPKParameter: AUC_tEnd ‚ÄĘ StartTime: 50 ‚ÄĘ EndTime: 80 ``` ::: ::: ## Application-Based Interval For multi-dose protocols, restrict the interval to specific administrations using application indices and offsets instead of absolute times: ```r myAUC$startApplicationIndex <- 2 myAUC$endApplicationIndex <- 3 myAUC$startTimeOffset <- 0 # minutes after start application myAUC$endTimeOffset <- 0 # minutes after end application ``` ## Calculate User-Defined PK Parameters ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} pkAnalysis <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simulationResults) myAUC_result <- pkAnalysis$pKParameterFor( quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)", pkParameter = "MyAUC" ) myAUC_result ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` ‚ÄĘ Name: MyAUC ‚ÄĘ QuantityPath: Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood) ‚ÄĘ Dimension: AUC (molar) ‚ÄĘ Unit: ¬Ķmol*min/l ``` ::: ::: . . . ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} myAUC_result$values ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 369.5769 ``` ::: ::: ## Exercise Overview Practice PK parameter analysis on the Aciclovir simulation: - **Plasma PK.** Get `C_max` and `C_tEnd` for plasma concentrations. - **Add whole blood output.** Repeat for whole blood concentrations after adding the output. - **Alternate dose.** Build a 100 mg simulation and export its PK table. - **Custom parameters.** Define time-bounded `C_max` / `C_min` and compute ratio. ## Baseline PK Let's practice! Start by accessing and saving into an object the value of `C_max` and `C_tEnd` for the Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood). ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} library(ospsuite) # Simulation Setup simExercise <- loadSimulation( system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite"), loadFromCache = FALSE ) simExercise_res <- runSimulations(simExercise)[[1]] # We create the PK analysis pkAnalysisExercise <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simExercise_res) # And access our PK parameters of interest C_maxExercise <- pkAnalysisExercise$pKParameterFor( quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)", pkParameter = "C_max" )$values C_tEndExercise <- pkAnalysisExercise$pKParameterFor( quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)", pkParameter = "C_tEnd" )$values ``` ::: ## Whole Blood Concentration Repeat the process but with the following output: `"Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Whole Blood (Peripheral Venous Blood)"` ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} # We do the same for another output of our choice, here the whole blood # First, we need to add the output to the simulation, and re run it addOutputs( "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Whole Blood (Peripheral Venous Blood)", simulation = simExercise ) simExercise_res <- runSimulations(simExercise)[[1]] pkAnalysisExercise <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simExercise_res) C_maxExercise_wholeBlood <- pkAnalysisExercise$pKParameterFor( quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Whole Blood (Peripheral Venous Blood)", pkParameter = "C_max" )$values C_tendExercise_wholeBlood <- pkAnalysisExercise$pKParameterFor( quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Whole Blood (Peripheral Venous Blood)", pkParameter = "C_tEnd" )$values ``` ::: ## 100 mg Simulation Create a new simulation with a dose of 100 mg, run it, then create a data frame called `pkAnalysisDF_100mg` containing all PK parameters of the Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood). Export the PK analysis to a csv file. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Reminder"} # Simulation Setup simFilePath <- system.file("extdata", "Aciclovir.pkml", package = "ospsuite") simDose100 <- loadSimulation(simFilePath, loadFromCache = FALSE) Dose100 <- getParameter( "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose", simDose100 ) setParameterValues(Dose100, 100, units = "mg") # We run the simulation simDose100_res <- runSimulations(simDose100)[[1]] ``` ::: ## 100 mg PK Data Frame + Export ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} pkAnalysis_100mg <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simDose100_res) # First, let's create the data frame and have a look at it pkAnalysisDF_100mg <- pkAnalysesToDataFrame(pkAnalysis_100mg) pkAnalysisDF_100mg ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} # Then, we export the PK parameters # Note: we can do both things independently exportPKAnalysesToCSV( pkAnalyses = pkAnalysis_100mg, filePath = "pkAnalysis_100mg.csv" ) ``` ::: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: ## Custom C_max and C_min Now, add two PK parameters, `C_max` and `C_min`, between 10 and 90 minutes, for the plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood). Then, compute the ratio `C_max/C_min` for the original simulation and for the new one with a dose of 100 mg. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} myC_max <- addUserDefinedPKParameter( name = "myC_max", standardPKParameter = StandardPKParameter$C_max ) myC_min <- addUserDefinedPKParameter( name = "myC_min", standardPKParameter = StandardPKParameter$C_min ) myC_max$startTime <- 10 myC_max$endTime <- 90 myC_min$startTime <- 10 myC_min$endTime <- 90 pkAnalysisExercise <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simExercise_res) pkAnalysis_100mg <- calculatePKAnalyses(results = simDose100_res) ``` ::: ## Ratio ‚ÄĒ Original Simulation ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} # We compute the PK parameters for the original simulation pk_cmax <- pkAnalysisExercise$pKParameterFor( quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)", pkParameter = "myC_max" ) pk_cmin <- pkAnalysisExercise$pKParameterFor( quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)", pkParameter = "myC_min" ) ratioExercise <- pk_cmax$values / pk_cmin$values ratioExercise ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 4.615281 ``` ::: ::: ## Ratio ‚ÄĒ 100 mg Simulation ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} # And for the new one pk_cmax_100mg <- pkAnalysis_100mg$pKParameterFor( quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)", pkParameter = "myC_max" ) pk_cmin_100mg <- pkAnalysis_100mg$pKParameterFor( quantityPath = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)", pkParameter = "myC_min" ) # The same ratio is obtained, but C_max and C_min values are different ratio100mg <- pk_cmax_100mg$values / pk_cmin_100mg$values ratio100mg ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` [1] 4.615281 ``` ::: ::: ## Resources - [PK Sim section about PK-Parameters](https://docs.open-systems-pharmacology.org/working-with-pk-sim/pk-sim-documentation/pk-sim-simulations#pk-parameters) - [`{ospsuite}` vignette](https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/articles/pk-analysis.html)

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
